description: The six invariants of the Warp Commerce Model as structured metadata. `enforcement_kind` tells a generator HOW the invariant is enforced. `rule` is a machine-readable expression where the invariant is datafiable (sums, uniqueness, ordering, table membership); where the invariant needs real logic, `prose_spec` is the precise specification each per-language generator implements, and `fixtures` lists the conformance scenarios that — not hand-written code — are the cross-language guarantee. Reference implementation: packages/commerce-types/src/invariants.ts.

enforcement_kinds.structural: Checkable from shape / arithmetic (a sum, a decomposition). E.g. I-1 conservation sums, MoneyBreakdown sum, I-6 tree sum.
enforcement_kinds.transition: Enforced by the transition table in behavior/transitions.json. E.g. I-2.
enforcement_kinds.sequence: An ordering rule over timestamps / causal order. E.g. I-4.
enforcement_kinds.precondition: A predicate that must hold before a specific transition is allowed. E.g. I-3 capacity before Accepted.

invariants.name: Value Conservation
invariants.description: Value is never created or destroyed by a commerce operation; it transfers (the originating party no longer holds it). Conservation has four clauses by ValueForm: physical/money (transfer), non-exclusive digital (access rights granted/revoked, provider retains copy, cannot grant more than licensed), exclusive digital (ownership transfers like physical; Retired extinguishes by mutual agreement), and loyalty/merchant-issued currency (value CREATION bounded by the issuer's total outstanding liability).

expressions.expression: distinct_currencies(money_values(subject.offered ++ subject.requested)).count <= 1  unless an explicit CurrencyConversion is recorded in terms.payment.currency_conversion
for breakdown b: all(c.amount.currency == b.total.currency for c in b.components) AND money_equals(sum(c.amount.amount for c in b.components), b.total.amount, b.total.currency)
expressions.notes: Discount components carry a negative amount and therefore subtract. Equality is checked to within the currency's minor-unit tolerance (moneyEpsilon), not exact float equality. This rule is the explicit extension of I-1 to MoneyBreakdown.

invariants.prose_spec: Conservation logic that is NOT a single sum (cross-transfer-type reasoning) must be implemented per language exactly as: (1) physical goods & money — after a Transferred transition the source party's holding decreases by exactly the quantity the destination's increases; nothing appears or vanishes. (2) non-exclusive digital — granting access does not reduce the provider's copy; a provider must never grant more concurrent/seat access than the license permits it to sub-license. (3) exclusive digital (NFT, unique certificate, carbon credit pre-retirement) — transfer follows the physical rule (originator loses the token); Retired is permanent consumption recorded by mutual agreement, NOT a transfer. (4) loyalty/merchant currency — issuance is controlled value CREATION; conservation applies to the issuer's aggregate outstanding liability, and redeemed points transfer normally (customer -> merchant) and are then extinguished.

invariants.name: State Monotonicity
invariants.description: CommitmentState and FulfillmentState transitions follow directed paths. A Fulfilled Commitment cannot return to Accepted; a Cancelled Commitment cannot become Fulfilled. The only apparent reversal (returning goods/money) is a NEW forward-moving Commitment with the parties exchanged, not a state change on the original.

expressions.expression: for each history entry h on a Commitment: h.to.type in transitions.commitment[h.from.type]
for each Intent transition: to.type in transitions.intent[from.type]
for each Fulfillment transition: to.type in transitions.fulfillment[from.type], EXCEPT Failed -> Planned which is valid iff from.recoverable === true

invariants.prose_spec: Enforced entirely by membership in behavior/transitions.json plus the documented Failed->Planned recoverable special case. No additional logic.

invariants.name: Capacity Verification
invariants.description: A Commitment cannot reach Accepted unless the capacity of all parties for their roles has been verified. Physical goods: inventory Available or Reserved with a verified basis (PhysicalStock/ProductionCapacity, not Speculative). Services: time slot confirmed. Digital: license entitlement verified. Financial: creditworthiness/guarantee confirmed. A Commitment MAY reach Accepted with Speculative reservation, but the basis MUST be recorded explicitly so capacity risk is computable.

expressions.expression: if reached_accepted(commitment) then initiator.capacity.can_buy === true (and the relevant role capacity for each party)

invariants.prose_spec: Precondition on the transition into Accepted. Generators must check the role-appropriate capacity flag(s) (can_buy for the initiator/buyer; can_sell/can_fulfill for the counterparty/fulfiller; can_guarantee for a guarantor) are true at the moment of acceptance. Speculative reservation is permitted at Accepted but the ReservationBasis must be present on the Reserved ValueState so downstream systems can compute capacity risk.

invariants.name: Temporal Integrity
invariants.description: Every state transition is recorded with a timestamp. No transition can have a timestamp earlier than any previous transition on the same object. History is append-only and immutable; corrections are new superseding entries, not modifications. Fulfillments execute only after their Commitment is Accepted.

expressions.expression: for each object O and consecutive history entries h[i-1], h[i]: timestamp(h[i].at) >= timestamp(h[i-1].at)
for each Fulfillment f of commitment c that is InProgress or Completed: accepted_at(c) is not null AND (f.started_at is null OR f.started_at >= accepted_at(c))

invariants.prose_spec: Ordering rule over ISO-8601 timestamps. Append-only history means a generator never mutates an existing entry; a correction is a new entry. Equal timestamps are allowed (>=, not >).

invariants.name: Identity Permanence
invariants.description: CommitmentID, FulfillmentID, IntentID, PartyID, and ValueID are globally unique and never reused. A platform's native order ID maps to exactly one CommitmentID; the mapping is established at creation and never changes.

expressions.expression: no identifier value appears more than once across the set of all PartyID, ValueID, IntentID, CommitmentID, FulfillmentID in scope

invariants.prose_spec: Uniqueness over the union of all identifiers. Reuse of any id (even across different primitive types, since ids are globally unique) is a violation.

invariants.name: Commitment Tree Consistency
invariants.description: For parent-child Commitment structures, the sum of all child Commitment subject.requested values (in their base currency) must equal the parent Commitment subject.requested value at all times. When a child is Modified (substitution, cancellation) the parent recalculates immediately. The parent's state reflects the aggregate state of its children.

expressions.expression: money_equals(sum(requested_total(child) for child in parent.children), requested_total(parent), base_currency) AND single_currency(parent + children)
expressions.notes: requested_total(c) sums the Money values in c.subject.requested. Equality uses the currency minor-unit tolerance (moneyEpsilon). Mixed currencies across parent/children without conversion is itself a violation. For exact splits that never drift, build children with allocate() (money.ts).

invariants.prose_spec: Structural sum over a parent and its present children. Recompute on any child Modified/Cancelled. If parent and children are denominated in different currencies, they must first be expressed in one base currency via explicit conversion before summing.
